notes: Two concurrent gemini REPL sessions launched in the SAME cwd (driver start_session; the 2nd shares session 1's directory, so no second trust dialog). Each gemini session writes its own chats/<sessionId>.jsonl, so the two distinct rows + independent working->ready arcs are observed correctly. The cell is KNOWN_FAILING because the daemon mis-binds PIDs: gemini's DiscoverPID hardcodes lowestPID and discards the PID-manager's claimed-aware disambiguator, so both same-cwd sessions resolve to the single lowest PID (cross-contaminated binding) -- the scenario's stable, non-cross-contaminated PID-per-session invariant fails until DiscoverPID honors the caller's disambiguator. The spec asserts the CORRECT lifecycle arc unchanged: (1) session 1 does an independent working->ready turn; (2) a DISTINCT session appears (s2_birth carries new_session:true) and does its own working->ready turn; (3) session 1 is driven AGAIN while session 2 is alive (s1_turn2_*, pinned same_session_as s1_turn1_start), proving its session binding stayed stable and never flapped to session 2. The first phase anchors on the first WORKING transition (not ready) so a transient proc-<PID> presession row born in ready can't greedily steal the birth.

text: Session 1 first prompt drives ready -> working (anchors the real sessionId; the pre-session proc-<PID> row never enters working)
Session 1 first turn completes: working -> ready
Session 2 starts in the SAME cwd as a DISTINCT session_id and appears in ready (new_session proves it is not session 1)
Session 2 first prompt drives ready -> working -- independent of session 1
Session 2 first turn completes: working -> ready

Session 1 is driven AGAIN while session 2 is still alive -- a working transition on session 1's original id proves the binding stayed stable and did not flap to session 2 (KNOWN_FAILING under the PID mis-bind: both rows share the lowest PID today)
Session 1 second turn completes: working -> ready
